hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions, while reducing off-target affinity by avoiding charge complementarity with SSTR3/5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating candidates with strong SSTR2 binding but insufficient selectivity. The focus must shift to tuning non-pharmacophore positions to break off-target interactions, particularly with SSTR3/SSTR5.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing on these positions allows us to tune interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 without compromising the pharmacophore core.
These mutations are designed to break conserved hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining flexibility and compatibility with SSTR2.
